question: After an operator selects an operation in the Operations panel on the Processes view, what change appears in the process instances list?

options.text: The list switches to a BPMN diagram view that highlights tokens affected by the operation.
A new column named Operation State is added to the processes instances list, showing the current state of the operation for each affected instance.
The list groups rows by BPMN flow node so operators can drill into specific elements.
The list becomes read-only until the selected operation finishes.

a.text: Selecting an operation does not replace the list with a diagram; the list stays as a list with an added column.

b.text: The docs state that after an operation is selected, a new column named Operation State is added to the processes instances list, indicating the current state of the operation.

c.text: Selecting an operation does not regroup the list by flow node; that is not part of the documented behavior.

d.text: The list is not made read-only by selecting an operation; only the Operation State column is added.

explanation: Selecting an operation in the panel both filters the processes list and augments it with an Operation State column that surfaces the per-instance status of the selected operation, so operators can scan completion and failure at a glance.
